frames.name: Batch configuration
textContent: Data trigger completes one batch and starts capturing data for the next batch
Correlates batches from one facility with batches from another using start/end time synchronization. Users can apply parameter-specific lag adjustments to align data accurately.

frames.name: Home
textContent:
It enables users to monitor key performance indicators (KPIs), track asset performance, and analyze trends...
Dashboard > Purpose

Plant data consists of multiple facilities, each containing assets. These assets, in turn, have multiple tags and KPIs.
Dashboard > Features & Functionalities > Plant data

Important Tags are part of the asset hierarchy and are accessible across the entire Mt Fuji platform.
Configurations > Data Sources > Purpose

The page provides an asset hierarchy structure, which allows users to navigate and manage manual and fixed data tags associated with specific assets.
Manual Data > Accessibility

frames.name: Draft 3 - batch view
textContent: Users can either switch between Parameter list or add parameters from “Add parameters” tab
Shows manually entered and top deviating parameters in comparison to reference batch
Users can either switch between Analysis window or filter parameters window
Parameters can be added from asset hierarchy or from the 2 selected batches.
All adjustments related to plotting of chart sit here.
All adjustments related to plotting of chart sit here.
Raw table data can be exported in excel format
Users can view either a chart of plotted parameter or raw table data.
All the adjustments related to analysis are done here.
Users can select between correlation and cross-correlation depending on whether there exists lag between event and its cause
Variable that shows deviation or an unwanted event for which the root cause is to be identified
Suggests the time period before the event where cause might have occured.
Similar events can be searched which meet the analysis filter
CTA that will take the input from analysis filters and perform RCA on the selected parameter
Displays the trend on the selected parameter
Displays the result of RCA performed.
This switcher will let users select a particular parameter from asset hierarchy or from either of the selected batch.
Top deviating parameters sorted according to the type of analysis selected are displayed here. Every row contains parameters from the 2 batches on which the following actions can be performed.
Top deviating parameters sorted according to the type of analysis selected are displayed here. Every row contains parameters from the 2 batches on which the following actions can be performed.
Entry point for user to enter batch view to either check what went wrong with a particular batch or for general analysis between 2 batches.
Users can view all the batches and their key information. users can sort, search and create new batch comparision.
To view batch 2 batch details, deviating parameters and chart view of selected batches
User can perform a new Root cause analysis or Exploratory analysis.
To perform RCA on selected parameters.
To perform RCA on selected parameters.
All the plant data, facilities, assets, tags and KPIs are stored here. Users can fetch data from this repository.
Uses statistical machine learning model to detect deviations.
Uses cluster machine learning algorithm to detect deviations.
Uses both statistical and cluster models
To filter the analysis type into Statistical, Cluster or Hybrid
Top deviating parameters sorted according to the type of analysis selected are displayed here.
To compare a selected batch with golden batch predefined by user

frames.name: Events
textContent: Entry point for user to enter Events module.
System lists all the events that have been created and their status.
Users can access trigger history for each of the event in events list. There will also be an option to edit or delete a particular event
CTA for the user to create new event.
CTA where user acknowledges the details mentioned above and proceeds to create/update event.
User can add recipients by either email or username.
interval in minutes at which notification will be sent again if the event is still ON or hasn’t been acknowledged.
This event will only be activated if it satisfies the conditions established here.
This event will only be activated if it satisfies the conditions established here.
This event will only be activated if it satisfies the conditions established here.
Basic event details will go here
User defines here what triggers the event
System notifications will be sent in case of triggered event. User has the option to add email notification if required.
Landing page for selected event history.
2 CTAs in every row of triggered event
Users get to see the event info, summary and chart view of the event triggered.
Overlay for user to acknowledge a particular event
